original: An emergency arctic localizer broadly used by Uveto's rescue teams. This one in particular is tuned to the Stormguard's frequencies, guaranteeing you to be saved by one of their warriors if you ever find yourself lost in a cave or if Uveto's blizzards put your life at risk.
context: <<POS:06765977>> guard Rescue Beacon",e.tooltip="An emergency arctic localizer broadly used by Uveto's rescue"

original: {var0}+{var1}+" uses the beacon to no effect.
context: <<POS:06766804>> <<VARS:var0=e.capitalA,var1=e.short>> output(e.capitalA+e.short+" uses the beacon to no effect.")

original: You open the Stormguard beacon and push the button. The device starts glowing and beeping softly, filling your surroundings with shimmering lights. Let’s hope the signal’s strong enough — you’re freezing! A handful of minutes later, a white shuttle with intricate lightning patterns soars near you, turbines revving. The shiny transport lands a few yards away from you. The rear panel opens a moment later, revealing a trio of heavily-armored and insulated rangers. “Orvil, search left! Erda, look to the right! Grimald, keep the engines hot!” a statuesque milodan blares, complementing his words with wide moves. The squad moves quickly. They scour their surroundings in a heartbeat, looking for any movement, listening to everything, even huffing the air to catch your scent. None of them seems bothered by the cold, not even Erda, the busty deer amazon. Orvil, the bearmorph, slowly approaches you. “Hey! Over here! Are you okay? Can you move?” You nod. “I’m fine, frigid but fine.” “Good,” the feline leader purrs. “Is there anyone else with you?” “No,” you reply. “Let’s get you back to Irestead then. Feels good to have a smooth recovery from time to time,” the hulking ranger groans. The three stormguards escort you back to their shuttle, stun guns raised, just in case. The coldness of the tundra soon disappears, replaced by the engines’ warmth then by the ship’s controlled climate. Grimald risks a glance behind. The half-anat sighs, relieved to see you in good shape. “When I saw your distress signal, I rallied my team and we came as fast as we could.” “Poor bird was all panicked and shaky,” the polar bear teases. He reaches for a thermos and serves you a cup of herbal tea. “Drink, it’ll warm you up.” “Yeah. I don’t know what’s between you two, but it means a lot to him,” Erda adds before claiming the seat across from you. You take a sip. The herbal infusion is sweet and honeyed, with an aftertaste of elderberries and blackberries. It’s almost liquorous — a hot, syrupy liquor. The team leader closes the hatch behind him. He’s not as physically expressive as his c[silly |u |o ]mrades but you can clearly see the relief in his eyes. “You’ll chitchat later. Grimald, make the engines scream! My fur’s been frosted enough for to[dayNight |day |night ].”

original: The Stormguards bring you back to Irestead in a heartbeat. You barely have the time to finish your drink before the shuttle lands next to the temple. Grimald flicks the switches off one after the other, deactivating the avionics, fuel pumps, and atmospheric shield as soon as the machine touches the snow-covered ice. “Dear passengers, we safely landed in Irestead. The external temperature is minus 10 and the current time is {time}. Thanks for choosing Stormguard Express. Please, use the ramp to get off board,” the half-anat playfully mumbles. “Grimald, we aren’t a damn airline!” the burly milodan grunts. “Well it shows. I’ve been on low-budget rides that had better food and movies,” Erda comments. Orvil yawns. “It’s nice to have a calm ride from time to time. I’ve stitched up enough unprepared adventurers for three lives already.” “We don’t get to choose who needs help, but it’s our duty to do our best,” Grimald reminds him before leaving the pilot’s seat. “Frozen, hurt, unconscious; it’s all the same in the end. We answer the call.” The stormguards surround you and escort you back to the temple’s gates. Their rations and infusion warmed your body and soul – you feel like you could climb mountains! It’s snowing slightly, the tiny snowflakes gently dance in front of your eyes, giving an ethereal air to the city. Not an hour ago, you were in the wild, battered by merciless winds, at the mercy of predators, and now here you’re in a postcard-like town, with [dayNight |children playing in the snow |people eating and drinking at food joints and bars ], leagues away from the dangers that lurked beneath the ice. “Be careful out there [pc.name]. This place’s a pile of problems,” Orvil says. How so? Irestead looks pretty calm compared to the glaciers. “Drunken brawls here, legs and arms broken after a fall there, [silly |the fat huskar that gets into a fight with Grimald’s father whenever they cross each other |tribal rivalries and infighting ], this place’s far more active than it seems. Tundras, ice seas, frozen caves, those are tame and safe in comparison,” Uther, the milodan, mumbles. “Whatever old man,” Erda teases. “See yah [pc.name]. Drop by if you want to go for a drink.” “Kids these days. Safe travels [pc.name] — and don’t stay out too long Grimald,” Uther groans before going back to the barracks with Orvil and Erda in tow. The half-anat smiles. “Don’t let his rough manners fool you. Uther’s got a heart of gold and a steadfast resolve. He’s just... not a wordsmith. Anyway, I’m glad that you’re okay. By the way, how was the tea?” “Pretty sweet. A bit too syrupy and hot if I’m honest.” “Oh! I need to adjust the recipe a little. The microsurgeons must have tampered with the consistency and gave a honey-like texture.” Wait! Was the infusion a remedy? Grimald smiles. “It’s nothing fancy but it gets things done. An old anat recipe to fight seasonal cold upgraded with some confederacy-grade microsurgeons. It should be as thin as water and with a hint of tanginess... Do you feel anything weird going on? A strange dizziness or stomach cramps?” No? You feel warm on the inside but that’s all. “All good then. I hope that you liked the ride. I tried to fly as lightly and swiftly as possible.” “[pc.isMischievous |You could have gone rougher and faster,” you playfully retort. |It was rather cozy – warm and cozy, unlike the icy plains,” you reply. ] The half-anat nods. “If you want to have more fun, I can take my break now. I could use a friend to warm me up.” You [flags.GRIMALD_SEX |blow him a kiss |shake your head ]. “Maybe later. The [dayNight |day isn’t over yet |Night is still young ].” “Have fun out there, [pc.name]. Come around if you want some hot chocolate [flags.GRIMALD_SEX |or a tight hug ].” Oh! You will.
context: <<POS:06769662>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tThe Stormguards bring you back to Irestead i'...])
